text: Layer 3 framing overstated — Tier 2-3 metadata (re, ts, ctx, sig, authz, tenant) overlaps A2A/NLIP envelope semantics
'Wide open' claim contradicts JSON lock-in evidence in the same document
TOON is secondary threat; JSON/NL inertia is the primary battlefield, not TOON
Option C alone is academic cul-de-sac without integration proof; recommend C+B hybrid
Token-cost thesis empirically under-supported on non-cost legs (reliability, ambiguity)
AISP omission from ecosystem positioning is a credibility gap given it's a 7th experimental condition
NLIP under-analyzed — ratified Ecma standard needs explicit coexistence mapping
Objective confusion: documents alternate between publishable research and adoption strategy without resolving hierarchy
Option E ranking too absolute — neutrality/utility tradeoff not engaged
Adoption economics under-modeled — no SDK, zero adoption, but implementation burden not quantified
Tier 1 routing semantics (@sender>@receiver) may still collide with A2A/NLIP addressing even after Tier 2-3 concession
Evidence category slippage: parseability ≠ reliability. Format conformance is prerequisite, not outcome evidence
C+B defense shrinks B to Future Work paragraph, which no longer de-risks irrelevance
Action items lack decision gates, deadlines, acceptance criteria — invites reframing without execution
